summary: Joel announces a new Visual Studio add-in for FogBugz that lets developers access their bug tracker from within the IDE. He also introduces the FogBugz API version 1.0, which enables third-party integrations. The API currently supports basic functionality like listing open cases, with more features planned for FogBugz 6.0. He mentions the add-in will eventually become a paid premium feature but is free during its initial launch.
key_points: FogBugz for Visual Studio integrates bug tracking directly into the IDE as a tool window
The new FogBugz API 1.0 was created to make the add-in possible, with expanded functionality planned for version 6.0
The add-in is offered free initially but will become a paid premium feature
Third-party developers are invited to request API features they need for their own integrations
money_quotes: In the long run, I think, FogBugz for Visual Studio will be available as a premium feature available for a small extra charge, but for the time being, since it's brand new, you can download it for free.
Because FogBugz has been shipping for a while without an API, we provide a couple of files you have to install on your FogBugz server to make the API work.
key_insight: Shipping a product API after initial launch enables an ecosystem of integrations, and offering new add-ins for free at first is a practical way to drive adoption before monetizing.
